---
applyTo: "**"
description: "Use when finishing meaningful tasks to preserve continuity and avoid repeating mistakes."
---

# Memory Loop Standards

## Always Capture Meaningful Work

For meaningful tasks, propose a concise `MEMORY.md` update before closing.

Include:

- What changed.
- Why it changed.
- Verification commands run.
- Pitfalls, caveats, or follow-up notes.

## Memory Quality Rules

- Keep entries short and factual.
- Prefer reusable lessons over one-off details.
- Record operational truths (commands, gotchas, environment behavior).
- Avoid sensitive values and credentials.

## Suggested Entry Template

```
## YYYY-MM-DD - Task Summary

- Changes: ...
- Reason: ...
- Validation: ...
- Notes: ...
```

## Continuity Expectation

- Use prior memory entries to avoid repeating failed approaches.
- When a previous approach failed, note the corrected strategy.
